segments.text: Only in Mumbai can a highway forget its own blueprint. This is officially the eighth wonder of the world. But it's actually a glitch in the matrix. From a distance, it looks like a masterpiece.
But I see a terrifying mistake which millions of people will notice every single day. See, it starts with a promise. Four wide lanes, smooth
concrete and full speed. You think the government finally solved the mira traffic. You feel fast, you feel efficient. Finally, the progress is here. But then the betrayal comes.
Without any merge signs or warnings, the four lanes suddenly become two. It's like the road ran out of budget midway.
One moment you're cruising and the next there's a concrete wall in front of you. But here is why I'm actually worried.
This is not a bottleneck. This is a death trap at night. If you're a new driver or don't know this glitch, you're driving 80 km an hour into a vanishing
lane. It turns a commute into a survival game. We don't need plot twist on our highways. We need safety. Share this to
